name: Iterative Two-Pointer Merge
summary: Use two pointers advancing through both lists simultaneously, always splicing in the smaller front node — O(n+m) time, O(1) space (no new nodes allocated).
visualizationIntent: init: show list1 remaining [1,2,4] top-left and list2 remaining [1,3,4] top-right, result [∅] bottom-center. compare: both l1_rem[0] and l2_rem[0] flash compared-blue simultaneously. splice: the winning node (green) visually moves from its source chain into the result tail — source chain shrinks by one, result chain grows by one. drain: once one list is exhausted, remaining nodes from the other list flow one-by-one into result. return: result chain shows the complete merged sequence [1,1,2,3,4,4].

lineExplanations.3: Copy list1 into a working array — we'll shrink this as we consume nodes.
lineExplanations.4: Copy list2 into a working array — same pattern for list2.
lineExplanations.5: Initialize the result array — this will hold the merged sorted sequence.
lineExplanations.6: splice_source tracks which list we just spliced from (drives the animation).
lineExplanations.7: splice_val holds the value just spliced (also drives the animation).
lineExplanations.8: While both lists still have nodes: compare their fronts.
lineExplanations.9: Compare the front of list1 to the front of list2 — smaller wins.
lineExplanations.10: List1's front is ≤ list2's front — record that we're splicing from list1.
lineExplanations.11: Peek the value we're about to splice for the animation label.
lineExplanations.12: Remove list1's front and append it to result — this is the splice.
lineExplanations.14: List2's front is strictly smaller — record that we're splicing from list2.
lineExplanations.15: Peek list2's front value for the animation.
lineExplanations.16: Remove list2's front and append it to result.
lineExplanations.17: List2 is exhausted — drain any remaining list1 nodes.
lineExplanations.18: Record splice source as list1 for the drain animation.
lineExplanations.19: Peek the value about to be drained.
lineExplanations.20: Remove list1's front and append it to result.
lineExplanations.21: List1 is exhausted — drain any remaining list2 nodes.
lineExplanations.22: Record splice source as list2 for the drain animation.
lineExplanations.23: Peek the value about to be drained.
lineExplanations.24: Remove list2's front and append it to result.
lineExplanations.25: Return the fully merged sorted list.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode and Knacktor both expect.
syntaxExplanations.2: def mergeTwoLists(self, list1, list2): the method; list1 and list2 are Python integer lists.
syntaxExplanations.3: list(list1) makes a shallow copy — we mutate l1_rem without touching the original.
syntaxExplanations.5: result = [] starts an empty list for the merged output.
syntaxExplanations.6: splice_source = None — will become 'list1' or 'list2' on each splice step.
syntaxExplanations.7: splice_val = None — will hold the integer value just spliced.
syntaxExplanations.8: while l1_rem and l2_rem: — truthy check; an empty list is falsy in Python.
syntaxExplanations.9: l1_rem[0] — index 0 peeks at the front without removing it.
syntaxExplanations.10: String 'list1' marks the splice source for the visualizer.
syntaxExplanations.11: l1_rem[0] peeks the front value (we'll pop it on the next line).
syntaxExplanations.12: l1_rem.pop(0) removes and returns the front element; result.append(...) adds it.
syntaxExplanations.13: else: runs when the if-condition is False (l1_rem[0] > l2_rem[0]).
syntaxExplanations.14: String 'list2' marks the splice source for list2's node.
syntaxExplanations.15: Peek list2's front before popping it.
syntaxExplanations.16: Pop list2's front and append to result.
syntaxExplanations.17: while l1_rem: — drains list1 if any nodes remain after the main loop.
syntaxExplanations.18: Still splicing from list1 during the drain.
syntaxExplanations.20: Pop and append the remaining list1 node.
syntaxExplanations.21: while l2_rem: — drains list2 if any nodes remain.
syntaxExplanations.24: Pop and append the remaining list2 node.
syntaxExplanations.25: return result — the complete merged sorted list.
